Se is mo generating zones (hatched) of Kerch-Taman region from instrumental, arch eos e is mo logical, and paleoseismolog- ical data, with epicenters determined from instrumental and macroseismic data, for 1800-2014 (Pustovitenko et al., 1989; She- balin and Leydecker, 1997; United Geophysical Survey, 2016); years of largest earthquakes are indicated. <image>Caption:
Spatial distribution of sea ice concentrations from monthly CDR (left panels) and GSFC (right panels) data in the an- nual minimum sea ice extent month, that is, September, for year 1987, 1997, and 2007, as an example of illustrating the decadal change (i.e., shrinking) of Arctic sea ice coverage. The white hole in the plots denotes the area not measured by the satellite sensor over the North Pole.
Question:
What grid type is used for data representation?
